interactions.summary: Jiang completes the authority/conformity model: secret society members imagine possession by higher powers and use authority plus group conformity to override ordinary moral resistance.
He proposes a violent thought experiment about abduction and dependency to explain how destroyed reality can produce depression, attachment, and Stockholm syndrome rather than vengeance.
He maps the same psychology onto secret-society hierarchy: ritual torture creates slaves, degrees, trusted middle people, and lifelong dependence on masters.

claims.claim: Secret society members can act against their ordinary psychology because they believe themselves possessed by or serving a higher power.
Authority and conformity are the two psychological mechanisms underlying secret-society power.
Secret society hierarchy depends on initiates who do not know the true purpose of the group and middle people who become trusted through participation in degrading rituals.
claims.temporal_scope: Jiang's structural model of secret societies

claims.claim: When a person's reality collapses, dependency can replace vengeance; Jiang uses Stockholm syndrome and arranged marriage as examples of attachment after dependence.

signature_moments.moment: A person can be told the line is a circle and end up believing he saw a circle.
signature_moments.source_phrase: he's actually convinced he saw a circle
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It gives the secret-society model a concrete mechanism: group reality can overwrite perception.

signature_moments.moment: Destroyed reality makes the victim cling to the destroyer.
signature_moments.source_phrase: she clings to anyone who is willing to support her
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This is the dark psychological hinge for Jiang's later account of initiation and slavery.

signature_moments.moment: Degrees of secrecy are degrees of slavery.
signature_moments.source_phrase: these initiates become their slaves for life
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It turns hierarchy from a status ladder into a dependency machine.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Jiang's paired psychological foundation for secret society obedience.
Used as Jiang's example of dependence and attachment after traumatic collapse.
People who want to join a secret society while remaining ignorant of its true purpose; after ritual dependence they become loyal instruments.

uncertainty_notes.note: ASR repeatedly renders 'secret societies' as 'six societies' and 'lender class' as 'Latin class'; normalize in summaries but avoid quoting those phrases.
